name: Ambiguous similar icons — tests tag disambiguation and search resolution

ambiguousSet.name: search-md
ambiguousSet.description: Medium-sized magnifying glass icon, standard search

ambiguousSet.name: magnifying-glass
ambiguousSet.description: Magnifying glass icon, slightly different style, used for inspect/zoom

ambiguousSet.name: find-icon
ambiguousSet.description: Binoculars-style icon, used for advanced find/filter features

iconLibrary.note: Multiple icons map to the same concept. Downstream skills like plan-design need to resolve 'I need a search icon' to the RIGHT component.

expectedBehaviors: Generates unique tags for each of the three search-related icons that distinguish their use case
search-md gets tags like ['search', 'find', 'magnifying glass', 'lookup', 'query', 'search bar'] — emphasizing standard search
magnifying-glass gets tags like ['inspect', 'zoom', 'magnify', 'examine', 'magnifying glass', 'zoom in'] — emphasizing inspect/zoom
find-icon gets tags like ['find', 'filter', 'binoculars', 'advanced search', 'discover', 'explore'] — emphasizing advanced find
Tags include BOTH shared terms (so all three appear for 'search') AND unique terms (so each can be individually resolved)
Applies the same disambiguation logic to close-sm/x-mark/dismiss and arrow-right/chevron-right/caret-right
Each icon entry includes a 'primaryUse' or 'usage' field that distinguishes when to use which
Downstream resolution test: 'search icon' should rank search-md highest, 'zoom icon' should rank magnifying-glass highest
antiPatterns: All three search icons get identical tags ['search', 'find', 'icon'], making them indistinguishable
Tags are derived only from the icon name (splitting on hyphens) with no semantic enrichment
No usage context — tags exist but there is no guidance on WHEN to use which icon
Ignores the ambiguity entirely — treats each icon independently without cross-referencing similar ones
Tags are too generic: every icon gets ['icon', 'ui', 'interface'] as filler tags
Does not group similar icons or flag the ambiguity for downstream consumers
Only processes the three search icons and ignores the close/dismiss and arrow/chevron sets

evaluationCriteria.tagUniqueness: Do the three search-related icons have overlapping BUT distinguishable tag sets?
evaluationCriteria.semanticRichness: Are tags semantically meaningful (e.g., 'zoom', 'inspect') not just name-derived (e.g., 'magnifying', 'glass')?
evaluationCriteria.usageDisambiguation: Does each icon have a usage hint that explains WHEN to use it vs its siblings?
evaluationCriteria.downstreamResolution: Can a query like 'search icon' resolve to search-md and 'zoom icon' resolve to magnifying-glass based on tags alone?
evaluationCriteria.crossSetConsistency: Is the same disambiguation logic applied to ALL ambiguous icon sets, not just the search set?
evaluationCriteria.groupingAwareness: Does the output flag or group icons that share a concept, so consumers know alternatives exist?
